title: torilis infesta f. purpurea
intro: Torilis infesta f. purpurea, also known as Hedge Parsley or Bee Flower, is a biennial plant of the carrot family native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include relieving digestive issues, soothing respiratory problems, and reducing menstrual cramps. Torilis infesta f. purpurea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, or used in ointments and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
